pages.title: User Funnel | User Engagement
pages.key_finding: In February, User Stickiness improved from 6.83% to 10.81% due to the drop in Monthly Active Users (from 82 to 37) outpacing the drop in Daily Active Users (from 5.7 to 4.0). Despite an excellent 89.1% registration conversion rate, the 54.9% drop-off before gameplay highlights critical onboarding friction that prevents new registrants from converting to active players.

pages.title: Game Performance (Score)
pages.key_finding: Average daily scores suffered a drastic 66.8% reduction from 87,248 in January to 28,986 in February. This sharp decline signals a major drop in competitive engagement and player progression, indicating users are either playing fewer rounds or struggling with the content.

pages.title: Game Performance (Time)
pages.key_finding: Average daily playtime plummeted by 63.2%, from approximately 87 minutes in January to just 32 minutes in February. This severe reduction in time spent in-game perfectly mirrors the drop in scores, pointing to a widespread loss of interest or potential technical hurdles affecting session lengths.
